name: Jared
shortDescription: Jared is a figure in the Hebrew Bible, the sixth in the ten pre-flood generations between Adam and Noah. He is known for his exceptionally long lifespan.
description: Jared, whose name means 'descent' or 'to rule,' is a significant figure in the genealogy presented in Genesis 5. He is described as the son of Mahalalel and the father of Enoch. The Hebrew Bible attributes to him a lifespan of 962 years, making him one of the longest-lived individuals mentioned in the scriptures. This extraordinary longevity is a characteristic shared by other pre-flood patriarchs and is often interpreted as a sign of divine favor or a reflection of a time closer to creation when the human condition was less subject to mortality. Beyond his place in the lineage, Jared's role is relatively limited in the mainstream biblical narrative. However, his importance grows in later Jewish traditions, particularly within the Enochic literature. The connection between Jared and his son Enoch, who 'walked with God' and was taken without experiencing death, further elevates Jared's status. Some interpretations suggest that the sinfulness of the world increased during Jared's lifetime, contributing to the eventual flood. The Book of Jubilees also mentions Jared and his role in teaching his descendants the proper ways of righteousness before the world turned wicked. His existence is a key element in understanding the antediluvian world and the gradual corruption that led to the great flood.
